steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must logically complete the phrase “raising the possibility that ______”. The passage has just noted that captive primate studies show significantly greater right-handedness than wild primate studies. The blank must name a possibility that explains that captive-versus-wild gap — not just comment on sample sizes or methodology.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the number of individuals in the study of wild chimpanzees is insufficient to preclude the claim that nonhuman primates exhibit PLRH.: This raises a concern about the wild study's sample size but doesn't explain why captive groups show more right-handedness than wild groups. The passage's observation is about a pattern across many studies, not one sample size problem. ✗
  • Choice B — the number of individuals in the study of captive gorillas is insufficient for a robust claim regarding evidence of PLRH.: This casts doubt on the captive study's sample size, but the passage says captive studies in general show more right-handedness — so questioning one study's sample size doesn't explain the broader captive-versus-wild pattern. ✗
  • Choice C — the greater exposure to humans among the captive gorillas induced them to acquire more right-handed behaviors.: This directly explains the captive-versus-wild gap: captive primates are around right-handed humans more, which could cause them to adopt right-handed behaviors. This is a logical causal mechanism for the observed difference. ✓
  • Choice D — the apparent discrepancy between the studies' results may be partly attributable to the 1963 study using different criteria when assessing hand dominance than were used in the 1993 study.: This proposes a methodological explanation for two specific studies, but the passage generalizes across many captive versus wild studies — different assessment criteria between two particular studies doesn't explain the broader, consistent pattern. ✗
That is the correct answer.
